statement.markdown: You are given a function `heapify` that implements the max heap property on the input array `nums`.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a sequence of integers `nums` of size `n`.
statement.outputFormat: The function should modify the input `nums` to satisfy the max heap property.

examples.explanation: The initial array is a sequence of increasing numbers

hints.text: Start by identifying the bug in the provided code.

editorial.title: Heapsort Insertion Bug Fix
editorial.intuition: Correctly implementing the heapify function to satisfy the max heap property.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves iterating over the entire array to check if it satisfies the max heap property.
editorial.betterSolution: Heapsort algorithms have a time complexity of O(n log n).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using the heapify function to ensure the max heap property is satisfied.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that the heapify function satisfies the max heap property.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the solution

functionSignature.java: public void heapify(int[] nums, int n);
